items.briefing:
Interpol has reported a sharp rise in cybercrime across Asia, a trend that puts Australian organisations on heightened alert given our regional connections. Separately, two new ransomware threats are making headlines: the "Gentlemen" gang is providing affiliates with tools designed to disable security software before launching attacks, while a new ransomware strain called "Prinz Eugen" targets your most recently modified files first, meaning active work documents are at greatest risk. Cybercriminals are also exploiting FIFA World Cup 2026 excitement through scams and phishing campaigns.

Be cautious of any World Cup-related emails, ticket offers, or links — if it looks too good to be true, don't click it.

featured.title: ACSC warns of reported credential exposure affecting Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways
featured.quote: ACSC warns of critical Fortinet firewall vulnerability affecting Australian infrastructure security directly.
